steps.title: Use Desmos to fit the table
steps.content: Enter three table points as $x_1=[0,3,6]$ and $y_1=[46,34,22]$. Then type the linear regression row $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=-4$ and $b=46$, so the line is $y=-4x+46$.

steps.title: Scale to match the answer forms
steps.content: The choices use coefficients of $24$ and $6$ (or $6$ and $24$). Multiply $4x + y = 46$ by $6$: $24x + 6y = 276$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A ($24x+6y=46$): $24(0)+6(46)=276 eq 46$, fails at $(0,46)$.
  • Choice B ($24x+6y=276$): all table points satisfy it, as shown above.
  • Choice C ($6x+24y=46$): $6(0)+24(46)=1104 eq 46$, fails immediately.
  • Choice D ($6x+24y=276$): $6(0)+24(46)=1104 eq 276$, fails at $(0,46)$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
